steps.title: Calculate angle L in radians by multiplying angle K by 12
steps.content:
First, simplify angle K:
$$K = \frac{\pi}{2 \cdot 12} = \frac{\pi}{24} \text{ radians}$$
Then multiply by 12 to find angle L:
$$L = 12 \cdot K = 12 \cdot \frac{\pi}{24} = \frac{12\pi}{24} = \frac{\pi}{2} \text{ radians}$$

steps.title: Convert from radians to degrees
steps.content:
Use the conversion factor: $\pi$ radians $= 180°$
$$L = \frac{\pi}{2} \text{ radians} = \frac{180°}{2} = 90°$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
